audit_items.proof_trace: The only pointer dereference in scoped_array.hpp is `px[i]` in operator[] (L63), which lacks an explicit null guard. However, this is intentional Boost-derived design: callers use operator bool/operator! to check null before indexing. All actual callers in the codebase (string_utils.cc) initialize scoped_array via `new char[...]` (non-null by construction) and exclusively call .get() — never operator[] — so no reachable null dereference exists. delete[] on null in the destructor is safe per C++ standard.
